pi_items.verified_value: Real Natural Earth dataset (ne_10m_airports v5.0.0) from https://naciscdn.org/naturalearth/10m/cultural/ne_10m_airports.zip
pi_items.source: Downloaded and inspected ZIP file contents; verified file exists in archive with correct name

pi_items.verified_value: Real Natural Earth dataset (ne_10m_urban_areas v4.1.0) from https://naciscdn.org/naturalearth/10m/cultural/ne_10m_urban_areas.zip
pi_items.source: Downloaded and inspected ZIP file contents; verified file exists in archive with correct name

pi_items.verified_value: 0.05 degrees = ~5.5 km at equator, ~3.9 km at 45° latitude
pi_items.source: Geographic calculation based on WGS84 Earth radius (~111 km per degree at equator); confirmed as reasonable buffer distance for noise analysis around airports

pi_items.verified_value: .model3 is the correct file extension for QGIS Processing Models in QGIS 3.x
pi_items.source: QGIS documentation and model designer specifications; path structure matches task setup_task.sh directory creation

pi_items.verified_value: GeoJSON is a standard, valid output format for vector layers in QGIS (save via Layer > Save As > GeoJSON)
pi_items.source: QGIS documentation and standard GIS workflows; verified that QGIS supports direct GeoJSON export

pi_items.verified_value: Segments parameter controls line segments approximating quarter circle in buffer operation; value of 5 is valid and commonly used
pi_items.source: QGIS buffer algorithm documentation; segments parameter is standard for buffer approximation

pi_items.metadata_value: Intersection or Clip algorithm connects buffered airports with urban areas
pi_items.verified_value: Both Intersection and Clip are standard QGIS vector overlay operations; Intersection creates output with overlapping portions from both layers

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real, publicly available Natural Earth datasets: ne_10m_airports (v5.0.0) and ne_10m_urban_areas (v4.1.0) downloaded from https://naciscdn.org/naturalearth/. The buffer distance of 0.05 degrees equals approximately 5.5 km at the equator, appropriate for noise pollution analysis. All claimed file formats (.model3 for QGIS models, .geojson for output) and algorithms (Buffer with 5 segments, Intersection) are standard, verified QGIS functionality. The data originates from official Natural Earth public domain sources, not synthetic data.
